Job Title: Python Lead Developer

Location: San Antonio, TX (Onsite)

Experience: 10+ years

Job Summary:

We are seeking a Data Engineer with strong Python expertise (primary) and DBT-based ELT experience (secondary) to support data ingestion, transformation, and pipeline development for Data Analytics and Monitoring solutions within the Pricing domain. This role focuses on building scalable, automated data pipelines and enabling high-quality, analytics-ready datasets.

Key Responsibilities:

· Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Python for ingestion, transformation, and processing.

· Implement and manage ELT workflows using DBT, ensuring modular, reusable, and maintainable transformations.

· Build and optimize Snowflake data models, views, and transformations for analytics consumption.

· Develop frameworks for handling structured and unstructured data ingestion.

· Ensure data quality, validation, and consistency across multiple data systems.

· Automate data workflows using Python-based utilities, SDKs, and CLI tools.

· Perform monitoring, troubleshooting, and performance tuning of pipelines.

Required Skills:

· Strong hands-on expertise in Python (Primary skill) for data engineering, including scripting, data processing, and pipeline development.

· Experience with DBT (Secondary focus) for data transformation and modeling.

· Solid understanding of ELT concepts, data pipeline architecture, and workflow orchestration.

· Strong experience with SQL and complex data transformations.

· Hands-on experience with Snowflake (data modeling, transformations, performance tuning).

· Experience integrating with ETL/ELT tools (Informatica, DataStage, or similar).

· Familiarity with data pipeline frameworks and orchestration tools.

· Experience with CLI-based tools and Python SDKs for automation and migration.

Preferred Skills:

· Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP) and modern data ecosystems.

· Experience with advanced DBT practices (macros, testing, documentation).

· Experience in Insurance domain, especially Pricing / Rating / Analytics datasets.

Strictly mention your visa status before sending your resume to

Mail: naveen@vsiontek.com

We are seeking an AI Engineer to develop and deploy AI-powered solutions focused on image processing and intelligent systems. This role involves integrating generative AI capabilities, and exposing models through scalable APIs and integrating them with tools.

Key Responsibilities

• Work with Generative AI pipelines using tools like Lang Chain and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

• Utilize commercial models (e.g., Qwen, GPT) for AI-powered features.

• Implement basic computer vision techniques, such as bounding box detection and object localization.

• Integrate AI models into applications and systems through scalable backend services.

• Prepare, clean, and manage image datasets to improve model performance and reliability.

• Build and maintain RESTful APIs using FastAPI to serve AI models.

• Store and manage data using cloud services such as AWS S3.

• Monitor, evaluate, and optimize model performance in production environments.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver AI-driven features.

Required Qualifications

• 1–2 years of experience in AI Engineering.

• Strong proficiency in Python.

• Hands-on experience with:

• Image processing and basic computer vision

• Building APIs using FastAPI

• Designing and working with RESTful APIs

• Experience with Generative AI frameworks (e.g., LangChain, RAG pipelines, OpenAI SDK or similar tools).

• Familiarity with AI models such as Qwen (via OpenRouter) or GPT-based models.

• Experience using AWS S3 for data storage.

• Knowledge of version control systems (Git).

• Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

Preferred Qualifications

• Experience with Docker for containerization.

• Familiarity with Redis for caching or fast data access.

• Understanding of deploying models in production environments.

• Exposure to machine learning practices and model lifecycle management.

• Knowledge with object detection frameworks (e.g., YOLO).

• Knowledge of deploying models to edge or resource-constrained devices.
